#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# Libraries
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
library(rvest)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)
library(glmnet)
library(explor)
library(FactoMineR)
library(ade4)
library(psych)
library(GPArotation)
library(httr)


#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# 1. Helper Constants
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

DEFAULT_REVIEW_CATEGORIES <- c(
  "Power", "Serves", "Control", "Groundstrokes", "Maneuverability", "Returns",
  "Stability", "Slice", "Comfort", "Topspin", "Touch/Feel", "Volleys"
)

DEFAULT_SPECNAMES <- c(
  "Length", "Head Size", "Weight", "Balance Point",
  "Construction", "Composition", "String Pattern"
)

# Additional hard-coded links in original code:
DEFAULT_ADDITIONAL_LINKS <- c(
  "https://www.tennis-warehouse.com/learning_center/racquet_reviews/PRGOSreview.html",
  "https://www.tennis-warehouse.com/learning_center/racquet_reviews/97V13Rreview.html",
  "https://www.tennis-warehouse.com/learning_center/racquet_reviews/PS97RFreview.html",
  "https://www.tennis-warehouse.com/learning_center/racquet_reviews/P97CVreview.html",
  "https://www.tennis-warehouse.com/learning_center/racquet_reviews/wblxbtreview.html",
  "https://www.tennis-warehouse.com/learning_center/racquet_reviews/hyigroreview.html"
)

# Corresponding additional model, brand, code
DEFAULT_ADDITIONAL_MODELS <- c(
  "Prince Original Graphite 107",
  "Wilson Pro Staff RF97 v13",
  "Wilson Pro Staff RF97 Autograph",
  "Wilson Pro Staff 97 Countervail",
  "Wilson BLX Blade Tour",
  "Head Youtek IG Radical OS"
)

DEFAULT_ADDITIONAL_CODES  <- c("PRGO", "97V13", "PS97R", "PS97CV", "WBLXBT", "HYIGRO")
DEFAULT_ADDITIONAL_BRANDS <- c("Prince", "Wilson", "Wilson", "Wilson", "Wilson", "Head")


#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# 2. get_tw_racquet_links
#    Scrape the main Tennis Warehouse page for racquet links and attributes.
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
#' @title Get Tennis Warehouse Racquet Links
#' @description Scrapes the Tennis Warehouse "reviewedracquets.html" page for
#'   initial sets of racquet model names, brand, code, and links.
#' @param url The URL to the Tennis Warehouse main racquet review listing.
#' @return A data frame with columns: \code{model}, \code{brand}, \code{code}, \code{link}
#' @title Discover Tennis Warehouse Racquet Reviews
#' @description Attempts to find all racquet review pages from the sitemap and the main
#'   reviews listing page, returning a tibble with (model, brand, code, link).
discover_racquet_reviews <- function() {
  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # 1) Discover links from the sitemap
  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  sitemap_url  <- "https://www.tennis-warehouse.com/sitemap.xml"
  sitemap_resp <- GET(sitemap_url)
  sitemap_links <- character(0)

  if (status_code(sitemap_resp) == 200) {
    sitemap_xml <- read_xml(content(sitemap_resp, as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8"))
    all_urls <- xml_find_all(sitemap_xml, ".//loc") %>% xml_text()

    # Filter for URLs that contain /learning_center/racquet_reviews/
    sitemap_links <- all_urls[str_detect(all_urls, "/learning_center/racquet_reviews/")]
  } else {
    message("Sitemap not found or inaccessible.")
  }

  # We'll create a tibble for these. We don't have brand/model from the sitemap,
  # so set them to NA. We can parse the 'code' from the URL, but keep in mind
  # the substring might not match the gtm_impression_code from the main page exactly.
  df_sitemap <- tibble(
    model = NA_character_,
    brand = NA_character_,
    # Example code extraction (if you want a code column):
    code  = str_extract(sitemap_links, "(?<=racquet_reviews/)[^/]+(?=.html)"),
    link  = sitemap_links
  )

  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # 2) Scrape the main "reviewedracquets.html" page for brand, model, code
  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  main_page_url <- "https://www.tennis-warehouse.com/reviewedracquets.html"
  main_page <- tryCatch(read_html(main_page_url), error = function(e) NULL)

  df_main <- tibble(model = character(0), brand = character(0), code = character(0), link = character(0))

  if (!is.null(main_page)) {
    # Extract brand, model, code from data-gtm_impression attributes
    impression_nodes <- main_page %>% html_nodes("div.cattable-wrap-cell.gtm_impression")

    brands <- impression_nodes %>% html_attr("data-gtm_impression_brand")
    models <- impression_nodes %>% html_attr("data-gtm_impression_name")
    codes  <- impression_nodes %>% html_attr("data-gtm_impression_code")

    # Extract actual links from the relevant container
    link_nodes <- main_page %>%
      html_nodes("div.cattable-wrap-cell-imgwrap.is-racquet") %>%
      html_nodes("a") %>%
      html_attr("href")

    links <- paste0("https://www.tennis-warehouse.com", link_nodes)

    # Combine into a tibble
    df_main <- tibble(
      model = models,
      brand = brands,
      code  = codes,
      link  = links
    )
  } else {
    message("Failed to scrape main reviews page.")
  }

  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # 3) Combine both sets of results, remove duplicates, clean the 'link' column
  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  df_out <- bind_rows(df_sitemap, df_main) %>%
    mutate(
      # Remove any carriage returns/newlines from link,
      # then trim any leading/trailing whitespace.
      link = link %>%
        str_replace_all("[\r\n]", "") %>%
        str_trim()
    ) %>%
    distinct(link, .keep_all = TRUE)

  return(df_out)
}

#' @title Get Tennis Warehouse Racquet Links from CSV
#' @description Reads racquet data from a CSV file instead of scraping the Tennis Warehouse website.
#' @param filename A string specifying the path to the CSV file.
#' @return A tibble/data frame with columns: \code{model}, \code{brand}, \code{code}, \code{link}
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#'   # Example usage:
#'   # get_tw_racquet_links_from_csv("racquets.csv")
#' }
get_tw_racquet_links_from_csv <- function(filename) {
  # Ensure required packages are loaded
  library(readr)
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)

  # Read the CSV into a data frame/tibble
  df <- read_csv(filename, show_col_types = FALSE)

  # The CSV is assumed to have columns Brand, Model, and Review Page URL
  # Rename columns to match the output specification (model, brand, code, link)
  df <- df %>%
    rename(
      brand = Brand,
      model = Model,
      link  = `Review Page URL`
    ) %>%
    # Create a code column by parsing the link.
    # This extracts everything between "racquet_reviews/" and ".html".
    mutate(
      code = str_extract(link, "(?<=racquet_reviews/)[^/]+(?=.html)")
    ) %>%
    select(model, brand, code, link)

  df
}


#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# 3. append_additional_links
#    Append any additional hard-coded or user-supplied links to the main data.
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
#' @title Append Additional Racquet Links
#' @description Augments the existing link data frame with manually specified
#'   racquet reviews (not listed in the main page).
#' @param df A data frame from \code{get_tw_racquet_links} with columns:
#'   \code{model}, \code{brand}, \code{code}, \code{link}.
#' @param additional_links Vector of URLs for extra racquets.
#' @param additional_models Vector of model names for extra racquets.
#' @param additional_brands Vector of brand names for extra racquets.
#' @param additional_codes Vector of codes for extra racquets.
#' @return A combined data frame with appended rows.
append_additional_links <- function(
    df,
    additional_links  = DEFAULT_ADDITIONAL_LINKS,
    additional_models = DEFAULT_ADDITIONAL_MODELS,
    additional_brands = DEFAULT_ADDITIONAL_BRANDS,
    additional_codes  = DEFAULT_ADDITIONAL_CODES
) {
  # Create a tibble from additional specs
  df_extra <- tibble(
    model = additional_models,
    brand = additional_brands,
    code  = additional_codes,
    link  = additional_links
  )

  # Combine
  bind_rows(df, df_extra)
}

# A helper function that cleans the URL and retrieves the HTML document.
get_clean_html <- function(link) {
  # Remove any carriage returns or newlines from the URL
  cleaned_link <- str_trim(str_replace_all(link, "[\r\n]", ""))

  # Use httr::GET with a custom User-Agent
  response <- GET(cleaned_link, add_headers("User-Agent" = "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64)"))

  # Check if the request succeeded
  if (status_code(response) == 200) {
    read_html(response)
  } else {
    message("Failed to retrieve: ", cleaned_link)
    NULL
  }
}


#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# 4. parse_racquet_review
#    Given a single racquet review URL & info, scrape all relevant data.
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
#' @title Parse a Single Racquet Review Page
#' @description Scrapes the tennis-warehouse racquet review page for overall
#'   rating scores, individual testers' scores, specs, etc.
#'
#' @param link Full URL to the review page.
#' @param model Model name of the racquet.
#' @param brand Brand name of the racquet.
#' @param code  Some unique code for the racquet (e.g. product code).
#' @param review_categories The categories in which the racquet is rated (default includes
#'   "Power", "Serves", etc.).
#' @param specnames Vector of specification labels to look for (default includes
#'   length, head size, weight, etc.).
#'
#' @return A \code{tibble} with columns:
#'   \item{player}{player/tester name}
#'   \item{category}{category of rating (e.g. "Power", "Control", etc.)}
#'   \item{score}{score from 1-10 or so}
#'   \item{model, code, brand}{metadata for the racquet}
#'   \item{flex, swingweight}{RDC machine flex rating and swingweight}
#'   \item{length, head_size, weight, balance, construction, material, string_pattern}{extracted specs}
old_parse_racquet_review <- function(
    link,
    model,
    brand,
    code,
    review_categories = DEFAULT_REVIEW_CATEGORIES,
    specnames         = DEFAULT_SPECNAMES
) {
  message(blue("==== Starting parse_racquet_review for link:"), green(link))

  # Use get_clean_html() to clean the URL and retrieve the HTML document.
  doc <- get_clean_html(link)
  if (is.null(doc)) {
    message(red("Failed to retrieve HTML for link:"), link)
    return(tibble())
  }

  #---------------------- 4.1 Retrieve Basic Scores --------------------------------
  message(yellow("Extracting basic scores using primary selector..."))
  snames <- doc %>% html_nodes("div.scores tr th") %>% html_text()
  svals  <- doc %>% html_nodes("div.scores tr td") %>% html_text()

  if (length(snames) == 0) {
    message(yellow("Primary basic scores not found. Trying fallback (div.review_scores)..."))
    sall <- doc %>% html_nodes("div.review_scores tr td") %>% html_text()
    if (length(sall) != 0) {
      snames <- sall[seq(1, length(sall), by = 2)]
      svals  <- sall[seq(2, length(sall), by = 2)]
    }
  }

  if (length(snames) == 0) {
    message(yellow("Fallback 1 failed. Trying fallback (div.scores table)..."))
    sall <- doc %>% html_nodes("div.scores table tr td") %>% html_text()
    if (length(sall) != 0) {
      snames <- sall[seq(1, length(sall), by = 2)]
      svals  <- sall[seq(2, length(sall), by = 2)]
    }
    if (length(snames) == 0) {
      message(yellow("Fallback 2 failed. Trying fallback (div.score_box)..."))
      sall <- doc %>% html_nodes("div.score_box tr td") %>% html_text()
      if (length(sall) != 0) {
        snames <- sall[seq(1, length(sall), by = 2)]
        svals  <- sall[seq(2, length(sall), by = 2)]
      }
    }
  }

  message(green("Basic scores extracted:"), length(snames), " entries found.")

  #---------------------- 4.2 Retrieve RDC Ratings (Flex, Swing Weight) -----------
  message(yellow("Extracting RDC ratings..."))
  rdc_ratings <- doc %>%
    html_nodes("table.racquet_rate_table") %>%
    html_nodes("td") %>%
    html_text2(preserve_nbsp = TRUE)

  if (length(rdc_ratings) == 0) {
    message(yellow("Primary RDC ratings not found. Trying fallback (table td table font)..."))
    rdc_ratings <- doc %>%
      html_nodes("table td table") %>%
      html_nodes("font") %>%
      html_text2(preserve_nbsp = TRUE)
    ind1 <- grep("Flex Rating", rdc_ratings)
    ind2 <- grep("Swing Weight", rdc_ratings)
    rdc_ratings <- rdc_ratings[c(ind1 + 1, ind2 + 1)]
  } else {
    tval <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(rdc_ratings[2]))
    if (!is.na(tval) && tval < 80 & tval > 40) {
      message(yellow("Assuming RDC ratings are in positions 2 and 4."))
      rdc_ratings <- rdc_ratings[c(2, 4)]
    } else {
      message(yellow("Searching RDC ratings for labels..."))
      ind1 <- grep("Flex Rating", rdc_ratings)
      ind2 <- grep("Swing Weight", rdc_ratings)
      rdc_ratings <- rdc_ratings[c(ind1 + 1, ind2 + 1)]
    }
  }

  message(green("RDC ratings extracted: Flex = "), green(rdc_ratings[1]),
          green(" | Swing Weight = "), green(rdc_ratings[2]))

  #---------------------- 4.3 Retrieve Specs ---------------------------------------
  message(yellow("Extracting racquet specs..."))
  specs <- doc %>%
    html_nodes("table.racquet_specs_table") %>%
    html_nodes("tr td") %>%
    html_text()

  if (length(specs) == 0) {
    message(yellow("Specs not found with primary selector. Trying alternative selector..."))
    specs <- doc %>%
      html_nodes("#main_content > center > table > tr > td:nth-child(1)") %>%
      html_text2(preserve_nbsp = TRUE)
    specs <- try(str_split(specs, "[\t\n]")[[1]], silent = TRUE)
    if (inherits(specs, "try-error") || is.null(specs)) {
      message(yellow("Alternative selector failed. Trying (center > table > tr > td:nth-child(1))"))
      specs <- doc %>%
        html_nodes("center > table > tr > td:nth-child(1)") %>%
        html_text2(preserve_nbsp = TRUE)
      specs <- try(str_split(specs, "[\t\n]")[[1]], silent = TRUE)
    }
  }

  message(green("Specs extracted, length: "), green(length(specs)))

  #---------------------- 4.4 Individual Player Scores -----------------------------
  message(yellow("Extracting individual tester scores..."))
  indiv <- doc %>%
    html_nodes("div.individ_scores") %>%
    html_nodes("div.tablewrap") %>%
    html_nodes("table")
  if (length(indiv) == 0) {
    message(yellow("No tables found with primary individual tester selector. Trying fallback (.tablewrap)..."))
    indiv <- doc %>% html_nodes(".tablewrap") %>% html_nodes("table")
  }

  message(green("Found "), green(length(indiv)), green(" individual score tables."))

  indiv_data <- lapply(indiv, function(x) {
    sname <- x %>% html_nodes("tr th") %>% html_text()
    sname <- iconv(sname, from = 'UTF-8', to = 'ASCII//TRANSLIT')
    sname <- gsub("Chris' Scores", "Chris", sname)
    sname <- gsub("Tiffani' Scores", "Tiffani", sname)
    sname <- gsub("James' Scores", "James", sname)
    sname <- gsub("Z'sofia", "Zsofia", sname)
    sname <- gsub("'$", "", sname)
    sname <- str_trim(gsub("'s Scores", "", sname))

    message(blue("Parsing individual scores for tester: "), sname)

    sval <- x %>% html_nodes("tr td") %>% html_text()
    scores <- sapply(review_categories, function(cat) {
      gind <- grep(cat, sval)
      if (length(gind) > 0) {
        sval[gind + 1]
      } else {
        NA
      }
    })

    tibble(
      player   = sname,
      category = review_categories,
      score    = scores
    )
  }) %>% bind_rows()

  #---------------------- 4.5 Clean up Specs, match "specnames" --------------------
  message(yellow("Cleaning up specs..."))
  specvals <- NA
  if (!is.null(specs) && !inherits(specs, "try-error")) {
    if (length(specs) == 10) {
      spind <- grep("/", specs)
      construction <- grep("mm", specs)[1]
      specvals <- specs[c(1, 3, 5, 7, construction, construction + 1, spind[length(spind)])]
    } else if (length(specs) == 11) {
      construction <- grep("mm", specs)[1]
      spind <- grep("/", specs)
      specvals <- specs[c(1, 3, 5, 7, construction, construction + 1, spind[length(spind)])]
    } else {
      specvals <- lapply(specnames, function(sp) {
        gind <- grep(sp, specs)
        if (length(gind) > 0) {
          specs[gind + 1]
        } else {
          NA
        }
      })
    }
  }

  if (length(specvals) != 7) {
    tmp <- rep(NA, 7)
    tmp[seq_len(min(7, length(specvals)))] <- specvals[seq_len(min(7, length(specvals)))]
    specvals <- tmp
  }

  message(green("Spec values cleaned."))

  #---------------------- 4.6 Build final data -------------------------------------
  if (length(rdc_ratings) < 2) {
    rdc_ratings <- rep(NA, 2)
  }

  # Convert flex and swingweight to numeric to ensure consistency
  result <- indiv_data %>%
    mutate(
      model = model,
      code = code,
      brand = brand,
      flex = as.numeric(rdc_ratings[1]),
      swingweight = as.numeric(rdc_ratings[2]),
      length = specvals[[1]],
      head_size = specvals[[2]],
      weight = specvals[[3]],
      balance = specvals[[4]],
      construction = specvals[[5]],
      material = specvals[[6]],
      string_pattern = specvals[[7]]
    )

  message(blue("==== Finished parsing link:"), green(link))
  return(result)
}


#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# 4. parse_racquet_review
#    Given a single racquet review URL & info, scrape all relevant data.
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
#' @title Parse a Single Racquet Review Page
#' @description Scrapes the tennis-warehouse racquet review page for overall
#'   rating scores, individual testers' scores, specs, etc. If the older style
#'   layout fails, tries the newer layout (as used on pages like YV98Preview.html).
#'
#' @param link Full URL to the review page.
#' @param model Model name of the racquet.
#' @param brand Brand name of the racquet.
#' @param code  Some unique code for the racquet (e.g. product code).
#' @param review_categories The categories in which the racquet is rated
#'   (default includes "Power", "Serves", etc.). (Used mainly for older pages.)
#' @param specnames Vector of specification labels to look for (default includes
#'   length, head size, weight, etc.). (Used mainly for older pages.)
#'
#' @return A \code{tibble} with columns:
#'   \item{player}{player/tester name}
#'   \item{category}{category of rating (e.g. "Power", "Control", etc.)}
#'   \item{score}{score from 1-10 or so}
#'   \item{model, code, brand}{metadata for the racquet}
#'   \item{flex, swingweight}{RDC machine flex rating and swingweight}
#'   \item{length, head_size, weight, balance, construction, material, string_pattern}{extracted specs}
#'
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#'   df <- parse_racquet_review("https://www.tennis-warehouse.com/learning_center/racquet_reviews/YV98Preview.html",
#'                              model="Yonex VCORE 98+ Racquet",
#'                              brand="Yonex",
#'                              code="YV98P")
#' }
#'
parse_racquet_review <- function(
    link,
    model,
    brand,
    code,
    review_categories = DEFAULT_REVIEW_CATEGORIES,
    specnames         = DEFAULT_SPECNAMES
) {
  message(blue("==== Starting parse_racquet_review for link:"), green(link))

  # Use get_clean_html() to clean the URL and retrieve the HTML document.
  doc <- get_clean_html(link)
  if (is.null(doc)) {
    message(red("Failed to retrieve HTML for link:"), link)
    return(tibble())
  }

  #------------------------------------------------------------
  # 1) Attempt OLD-STYLE parse as in your original code
  #------------------------------------------------------------
  message(yellow("Extracting basic scores using primary selector (old style)..."))

  snames <- doc %>% html_nodes("div.scores tr th") %>% html_text()
  svals  <- doc %>% html_nodes("div.scores tr td") %>% html_text()

  if (length(snames) == 0) {
    message(yellow("Primary basic scores not found. Trying fallback (div.review_scores)..."))
    sall <- doc %>% html_nodes("div.review_scores tr td") %>% html_text()
    if (length(sall) != 0) {
      snames <- sall[seq(1, length(sall), by = 2)]
      svals  <- sall[seq(2, length(sall), by = 2)]
    }
  }

  if (length(snames) == 0) {
    message(yellow("Fallback 1 failed. Trying fallback (div.scores table)..."))
    sall <- doc %>% html_nodes("div.scores table tr td") %>% html_text()
    if (length(sall) != 0) {
      snames <- sall[seq(1, length(sall), by = 2)]
      svals  <- sall[seq(2, length(sall), by = 2)]
    }
    if (length(snames) == 0) {
      message(yellow("Fallback 2 failed. Trying fallback (div.score_box)..."))
      sall <- doc %>% html_nodes("div.score_box tr td") %>% html_text()
      if (length(sall) != 0) {
        snames <- sall[seq(1, length(sall), by = 2)]
        svals  <- sall[seq(2, length(sall), by = 2)]
      }
    }
  }
  oldstyle_count <- length(snames)
  message(green("Old-style basic scores extracted:"), oldstyle_count, " entries found.")

  # Attempt to build old-style table if possible
  old_style_data <- tibble(player="TW Staff",
                           category=snames,
                           score=svals)

  #---------------------- Old-style RDC ratings -----------
  message(yellow("Extracting old-style RDC ratings..."))
  rdc_ratings <- doc %>%
    html_nodes("table.racquet_rate_table") %>%
    html_nodes("td") %>%
    html_text2(preserve_nbsp = TRUE)

  if (length(rdc_ratings) == 0) {
    message(yellow("Primary RDC ratings not found. Trying fallback (table td table font)..."))
    rdc_ratings <- doc %>%
      html_nodes("table td table") %>%
      html_nodes("font") %>%
      html_text2(preserve_nbsp = TRUE)
    ind1 <- grep("Flex Rating", rdc_ratings)
    ind2 <- grep("Swing Weight", rdc_ratings)
    if (length(ind1) && length(ind2)) {
      rdc_ratings <- rdc_ratings[c(ind1 + 1, ind2 + 1)]
    }
  } else {
    tval <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(rdc_ratings[2]))
    if (!is.na(tval) && tval < 80 & tval > 40) {
      # Assume RDC ratings in positions 2 and 4
      if (length(rdc_ratings) >= 4) {
        rdc_ratings <- rdc_ratings[c(2, 4)]
      }
    } else {
      message(yellow("Searching RDC ratings for labels..."))
      ind1 <- grep("Flex Rating", rdc_ratings)
      ind2 <- grep("Swing Weight", rdc_ratings)
      if (length(ind1) && length(ind2)) {
        rdc_ratings <- rdc_ratings[c(ind1 + 1, ind2 + 1)]
      }
    }
  }
  if (length(rdc_ratings) < 2) {
    rdc_ratings <- rep(NA, 2)
  }
  # For old style, parse specs from table.racquet_specs_table
  # (We keep your original fallback logic.)
  message(yellow("Extracting specs (old style)..."))
  specs <- doc %>%
    html_nodes("table.racquet_specs_table") %>%
    html_nodes("tr td") %>%
    html_text()

  if (length(specs) == 0) {
    message(yellow("Specs not found with primary old-style selector. Trying alternative selector..."))
    specs <- doc %>%
      html_nodes("#main_content > center > table > tr > td:nth-child(1)") %>%
      html_text2(preserve_nbsp = TRUE)
    specs <- try(str_split(specs, "[\t\n]")[[1]], silent = TRUE)
    if (inherits(specs, "try-error") || is.null(specs)) {
      message(yellow("Alternative old-style spec selector also failing."))
    }
  }

  # Attempt to parse old-style specs
  specvals <- NA
  if (!is.null(specs) && !inherits(specs, "try-error") && length(specs)>1) {
    # Your original snippet for old spec parse
    if (length(specs) == 10) {
      spind <- grep("/", specs)
      construction <- grep("mm", specs)[1]
      specvals <- specs[c(1, 3, 5, 7, construction, construction + 1, spind[length(spind)])]
    } else if (length(specs) == 11) {
      construction <- grep("mm", specs)[1]
      spind <- grep("/", specs)
      specvals <- specs[c(1, 3, 5, 7, construction, construction + 1, spind[length(spind)])]
    } else {
      specvals <- lapply(specnames, function(sp) {
        gind <- grep(sp, specs)
        if (length(gind) > 0) {
          specs[gind + 1]
        } else {
          NA
        }
      })
    }
  }
  if (length(specvals) != 7) {
    tmp <- rep(NA, 7)
    if (!is.na(specvals[1])) {
      # fill up what we can
      nn <- min(7, length(specvals))
      tmp[seq_len(nn)] <- specvals[seq_len(nn)]
    }
    specvals <- tmp
  }

  # Build old-style final:
  old_style_final <- old_style_data %>%
    mutate(
      model = model,
      code = code,
      brand = brand,
      flex = as.numeric(rdc_ratings[1]),
      swingweight = as.numeric(rdc_ratings[2]),
      length = specvals[[1]],
      head_size = specvals[[2]],
      weight = specvals[[3]],
      balance = specvals[[4]],
      construction = specvals[[5]],
      material = specvals[[6]],
      string_pattern = specvals[[7]]
    )

  # If old_style_data has 0 categories, we likely have an empty parse
  # Check how many unique categories we found:
  if (nrow(old_style_final) > 0 && any(!is.na(old_style_final$category))) {
    # We have data from old style
    message(green("Successfully parsed old style. Returning old_style_final."))
    return(old_style_final)
  }

  #------------------------------------------------------------
  # 2) Attempt NEW-STYLE parse if old style yields zero rows
  #------------------------------------------------------------
  message(yellow("No data from old style parse. Trying new style..."))

  # a) Try to parse "TWU Lab Data" for flex/swingweight
  flex <- NA_real_
  swingweight <- NA_real_
  lab_tbl <- doc %>% html_node("#table_data table.table-borderless")
  if (!is.null(lab_tbl)) {
    rows <- lab_tbl %>% html_nodes("tr")
    # each row: [ "Flex Rating", "66", "MEDIUM" ] or so
    for (rr in seq_along(rows)) {
      tds <- rows[[rr]] %>% html_nodes("td") %>% html_text(trim=TRUE)
      if (length(tds)>=2) {
        if (grepl("Flex", tds[1], ignore.case=TRUE)) {
          flex <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(tds[2]))
        }
        if (grepl("Swing Weight", tds[1], ignore.case=TRUE)) {
          swingweight <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(tds[2]))
        }
      }
    }
  }

  # b) Parse "Tech Specs" from #tech_specs #US
  length_ <- NA_character_
  head_size_ <- NA_character_
  weight_ <- NA_character_
  balance_ <- NA_character_
  construction_ <- NA_character_
  material_ <- NA_character_
  string_pat_ <- NA_character_

  us_table <- doc %>% html_node("#tech_specs #US table")
  if (!is.null(us_table)) {
    spec_rows <- us_table %>% html_nodes("tr")
    specs_df <- lapply(spec_rows, function(r) {
      tds <- r %>% html_nodes("td") %>% html_text(trim=TRUE)
      if (length(tds) == 2) {
        data.frame(name=tds[1], val=tds[2], stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
      } else {
        NULL
      }
    }) %>% dplyr::bind_rows()

    # match known fields
    if (nrow(specs_df) > 0) {
      getVal <- function(pattern) {
        idx <- which(grepl(pattern, specs_df$name, ignore.case=TRUE))
        if (length(idx)) specs_df$val[idx[1]] else NA_character_
      }
      length_       <- getVal("^Length")
      head_size_    <- getVal("^Head Size")
      weight_       <- getVal("^Weight")
      balance_      <- getVal("^Balance")
      construction_ <- getVal("^Construction")
      string_pat_   <- getVal("^String Pattern")
      # material is typically not listed in new style pages, so it remains NA
    }
  }

  # c) Breakdown summary staff line (#breakdown_summary table with categories)
  breakdown_tbl <- doc %>% html_node("#breakdown_summary table")
  breakdown_data <- tibble()
  if (!is.null(breakdown_tbl)) {
    b_rows <- breakdown_tbl %>% html_nodes("tr")
    breakdown_data <- lapply(b_rows, function(rr) {
      tds <- rr %>% html_nodes("td") %>% html_text(trim=TRUE)
      if (length(tds)==2) {
        # skip if it's "Final Verdict"
        if (!grepl("Final Verdict", tds[1], ignore.case=TRUE)) {
          tibble(player="TW Staff", category=tds[1], score=tds[2])
        } else {NULL}
      } else {NULL}
    }) %>% dplyr::bind_rows()
  }

  # d) Individual testers => .review-playtesters_section
  pt_sections <- doc %>% html_nodes(".review-playtesters_section")
  testers_data <- lapply(pt_sections, function(sec) {
    # parse name from:  <div id="BRITTANYB"><h2 class="mb-1">Brittany</h2>
    # or <h2>whatever
    name_node <- sec %>% html_node("h2.mb-1")
    if (is.null(name_node)) {
      name_node <- sec %>% html_node("h2")
    }
    p_name <- if (!is.null(name_node)) html_text(name_node, trim=TRUE) else "UnknownTester"

    # parse that small table below
    tab <- sec %>% html_node("table.table-borderless")
    if (is.null(tab)) return(NULL)

    rows_ <- tab %>% html_nodes("tr")
    out_ <- lapply(rows_, function(r) {
      tds <- r %>% html_nodes("td") %>% html_text(trim=TRUE)
      if (length(tds)==2) {
        tibble(player=p_name,
               category=tds[1],
               score=tds[2])
      } else {
        NULL
      }
    }) %>% dplyr::bind_rows()
    out_
  }) %>% dplyr::bind_rows()

  newstyle_data <- dplyr::bind_rows(breakdown_data, testers_data)

  if (nrow(newstyle_data)==0) {
    message(yellow("New-style parse also found 0 data. Returning empty tibble."))
    return(tibble())
  }

  # e) Build final
  out <- newstyle_data %>%
    mutate(
      model = model,
      code  = code,
      brand = brand,
      flex = flex,
      swingweight = swingweight,
      length = length_,
      head_size = head_size_,
      weight = weight_,
      balance = balance_,
      construction = construction_,
      material = material_,
      string_pattern = string_pat_
    )

  message(blue("==== Finished parsing link:"), green(link))
  return(out)
}
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# 5. scrape_all_racquet_reviews
#    Loop over all the racquet links, parse each one, and combine.
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
#' @title Scrape All Racquet Reviews
#' @description Iterates over a data frame of links, brand, model, code, calls
#'   \code{parse_racquet_review} for each, and combines the results.
#' @param df A data frame with columns: \code{model}, \code{brand}, \code{code}, \code{link}.
#' @return A single \code{tibble} with all racquet data combined.
scrape_all_racquet_reviews <- function(df) {
  # Print starting message
  message(crayon::blue("Starting to scrape all racquet reviews..."))

  # Step 1: Loop over rows and parse each review
  results_list <- vector("list", nrow(df))
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(df))) {
    message(crayon::green("Scraping "),
            crayon::cyan(i),
            " of ",
            crayon::cyan(nrow(df)),
            " : ",
            crayon::yellow(df$link[i]))
    results_list[[i]] <- parse_racquet_review(
      link  = df$link[i],
      model = df$model[i],
      brand = df$brand[i],
      code  = df$code[i]
    )
  }

  # Step 2: Bind the individual results into one tibble
  message(crayon::blue("Finished individual scrapes. Binding rows..."))
  results <- dplyr::bind_rows(results_list)

  message(crayon::blue("Scraping complete."))
  return(results)
}


#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# 6. compute_player_statistics
#    For each racquet, compute per-player "delta" from average, correlation to
#    average, global mean rating, etc.
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
#' @title Compute Player Statistics
#' @description For each model, splits the data by player and computes:
#'  \item{delta}{the sum of squared deviations from the model-level average}
#'  \item{cor}{Spearman correlation with the model-level average}
#'  \item{global}{the average rating across all categories for that player & model}
#' @param reviews A tibble with columns \code{model}, \code{player}, \code{score}.
#' @return A tibble with columns \code{player}, \code{delta}, \code{cor}, \code{global}, \code{model}.
compute_player_statistics <- function(reviews) {

  mnest <- reviews %>%
    nest_by(model)

  player_ret <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(mnest)), function(i) {
    dat  <- mnest$data[[i]]
    mod  <- mnest$model[i]

    # Build numeric matrix of category scores, row = player
    scores <- split(dat, dat$player) %>%
      map(function(x) as.numeric(x$score))

    players <- names(scores)
    smat    <- do.call(rbind, scores)
    smat    <- as.matrix(smat)

    # Fix any >10 scores
    smat[smat > 10] <- smat[smat > 10] / 10

    # Model-level average by column
    avg  <- colMeans(smat, na.rm = TRUE)

    # Sum of squared deviations from average (by player)
    delta <- apply(smat, 1, function(vals) sum((vals - avg)^2, na.rm=TRUE))
    # Spearman correlation with average
    cor   <- apply(smat, 1, function(vals) cor(vals, avg, method = "spearman", use="pairwise.complete.obs"))

    # Global average rating for each player across categories
    global <- rowMeans(smat, na.rm=TRUE)

    data.frame(
      player = players,
      delta  = delta,
      cor    = cor,
      global = global,
      model  = mod
    )
  }) %>%
    bind_rows()

  return(player_ret)
}


#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# 7. aggregate_player_stats
#    Summarize each player across all racquets.
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
#' @title Aggregate Player Statistics
#' @description Summarizes a player's \code{delta}, \code{cor}, and \code{global}
#'   across all racquets tested, then ranks the players with a \code{weightfac} metric.
#' @param player_ret A tibble from \code{compute_player_statistics}.
#' @return A tibble with columns:
#'   \item{player}{player name}
#'   \item{global}{average rating across racquets}
#'   \item{cor}{average correlation with racquet-level average}
#'   \item{delta}{average sum of squares from racquet-level average}
#'   \item{count}{how many racquets tested}
#'   \item{weightfac}{a combined factor used to weigh the player’s scoring}
aggregate_player_stats <- function(player_ret) {

  player_ret_sum <- player_ret %>%
    group_by(player) %>%
    summarize(
      global = mean(global, na.rm=TRUE),
      cor    = mean(cor, na.rm=TRUE),
      delta  = mean(delta, na.rm=TRUE),
      count  = n()
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(cor)) %>%
    mutate(
      weightfac = (
        scale(0.5 * rank(cor))[,1] +
          0.5 * scale(1 / rank(delta))[,1] +
          2   * scale(rank(count))[,1]
      ) / 3
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(weightfac))

  return(player_ret_sum)
}


#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# 8. join_player_stats
#    Join the player statistics back onto the main data.
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
#' @title Join Player Statistics
#' @description Merges \code{player_ret_sum} back into the main \code{reviews}
#'   data so each row has \code{weightfac}, \code{global}, etc.
#' @param reviews The main reviews tibble.
#' @param player_stats A tibble from \code{aggregate_player_stats}.
#' @return The combined tibble with \code{weightfac} appended.
join_player_stats <- function(reviews, player_stats) {
  ret <- inner_join(reviews, player_stats, by = "player")
  return(ret)
}


#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# 9. compute_weighted_scores_by_model
#    For each model, compute weighted average category scores using the
#    'weightfac' as weights.
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
#' @title Compute Weighted Scores By Model
#' @description For each racquet model, aggregates individual player
#'   category scores using a weighted mean (weight = pnorm(weightfac)).
#' @param reviews_with_stats A tibble that includes \code{model}, \code{player},
#'   \code{score}, and \code{weightfac}.
#' @return A tibble with columns: \code{model}, \code{avgscore}, \code{category}.
compute_weighted_scores_by_model <- function(reviews_with_stats) {

  avg_scores <- reviews_with_stats %>%
    nest_by(model)

  res <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(avg_scores)), function(i) {
    dat <- avg_scores$data[[i]]
    mod <- avg_scores$model[i]

    # scores: row = player, col = categories
    scores_split <- split(dat, dat$player)
    scores_list  <- map(scores_split, ~ as.numeric(.x$score))
    wts          <- map_dbl(scores_split, ~ as.numeric(.x$weightfac[1]))

    # Convert weightfac to a range ~ (0.0 - 1.0) using pnorm
    wts <- pnorm(wts)

    # smat: each row is a player, each column is a category
    smat <- do.call(rbind, scores_list)
    smat <- as.matrix(smat)
    smat[smat > 10] <- smat[smat > 10] / 10  # fix out-of-range

    # Weighted average across players, for each category (column)
    # The tricky part is ensuring we line up categories. We'll assume each player
    # row is in the same category order. If not, we'd need to reorder carefully.
    # But from the nest_by approach, the row order is consistent across players.
    avgcat <- apply(smat, 2, function(vals) weighted.mean(vals, wts, na.rm=TRUE))

    # The categories come from the first block of data
    categories <- dat$category[1:ncol(smat)]

    tibble(
      model    = mod,
      avgscore = avgcat,
      category = categories
    )
  }) %>%
    bind_rows()

  res
}


#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# 10. finalize_data
#    Summarize the final average scores with relevant specs and produce a
#    "long" format tibble or any final arrangement needed.
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
#' @title Finalize Data
#' @description Joins the weighted average scores with racquet specs to produce
#'   a final per-model, per-category summary.
#' @param reviews_with_stats The original reviews data (with specs).
#' @param weighted_scores Weighted average scores from \code{compute_weighted_scores_by_model}.
#' @return A final tibble with columns:
#'   \item{model, category, score, code, brand, flex, swingweight, length, head_size, weight, ...}
finalize_data <- function(reviews_with_stats, weighted_scores) {

  finalavg <- left_join(reviews_with_stats, weighted_scores, by = c("model", "category")) %>%
    group_by(model, category) %>%
    summarize(
      score         = mean(avgscore, na.rm = TRUE),
      code          = code[1],
      brand         = brand[1],
      flex          = flex[1],
      swingweight   = swingweight[1],
      length        = length[1],
      head_size     = head_size[1],
      weight        = weight[1],
      balance       = balance[1],
      construction  = construction[1],
      material      = material[1],
      string_pattern= string_pattern[1],
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  # Return
  finalavg
}


#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# 11. Helper function to standardize numeric columns of final data
#    Example: adding thickness, mains, crosses, etc.
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
#' @title Post-process specs
#' @description Example function to derive extra numeric columns (e.g. thickness)
#' @param finalavg A tibble from \code{finalize_data}.
#' @return The same tibble with new numeric columns (thickness, mains, crosses, balance_inches).
post_process_specs <- function(finalavg) {

  out <- finalavg %>%
    mutate(
      thickness = {
        tmp <- str_split(str_replace_all(construction, "mm", ""), "/")
        sapply(tmp, function(x) mean(as.numeric(x), na.rm = TRUE))
      },
      mains = {
        tmp <- str_split(string_pattern, "/")
        unlist(lapply(tmp, function(x) {
          vals <- as.numeric(str_replace(x, "([a-zA-Z].*)", ""))
          vals[[1]]
        }))
      },
      crosses = {
        tmp <- str_split(string_pattern, "/")
        unlist(lapply(tmp, function(x) {
          vals <- as.numeric(str_replace(x, "([a-zA-Z].*)", ""))
          vals[[2]]
        }))
      },
      balance_inches = {
        bp2 <- str_split(balance, "in")
        bp2 <- lapply(bp2, `[[`, 1)
        as.numeric(bp2)
      }
    )

  out
}


#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# Example Main Script
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

# 1) Get main page links
#racquet_df <- get_tw_racquet_links_from_csv("racquets.csv")
racquet_df <- discover_racquet_reviews()
# 2) Append any additional (hard-coded) links
racquet_df <- append_additional_links(racquet_df)

# 3) Scrape all reviews into one table
reviews <- scrape_all_racquet_reviews(racquet_df)

# 4) Compute player-level statistics (delta, cor, global)
player_ret <- compute_player_statistics(reviews)

# 5) Aggregate those player stats across all racquets
player_ret_sum <- aggregate_player_stats(player_ret)

# 6) Join them back into the main table
reviews_with_stats <- join_player_stats(reviews, player_ret_sum)

# 7) Compute weighted average category scores for each racquet model
weighted_scores <- compute_weighted_scores_by_model(reviews_with_stats)

# 8) Build final data with specs
finalavg <- finalize_data(reviews_with_stats, weighted_scores)

# 9) Optionally post-process specs (thickness, mains, crosses, balance_inches, etc.)
finalavg <- post_process_specs(finalavg)

# finalavg now contains the aggregated & processed data
# at the (model, category) level, plus derived numeric specs.

# You can now proceed with modeling or other analysis:
# e.g., correlation analyses, PCA, factor analysis, GLMNET modeling, etc.

1→# Install/load required packages
     2→if(!require(httr)) install.packages("httr")
     3→if(!require(rvest)) install.packages("rvest")
     4→if(!require(xml2)) install.packages("xml2")
     5→if(!require(stringr)) install.packages("stringr")
     6→if(!require(dplyr)) install.packages("dplyr")
     7→library(purrr)
     8→
     9→
    10→#' @title Discover Tennis Warehouse Racquet Reviews
    11→#' @description Attempts to find all racquet review pages from the sitemap and the main
    12→#'   reviews listing page, returning a tibble with (model, brand, code, link).
    13→discover_racquet_reviews <- function() {
    14→  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15→  # 1) Discover links from the sitemap
    16→  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→  sitemap_url  <- "https://www.tennis-warehouse.com/sitemap.xml"
    18→  sitemap_resp <- GET(sitemap_url)
    19→  sitemap_links <- character(0)
    20→
    21→  if (status_code(sitemap_resp) == 200) {
    22→    sitemap_xml <- read_xml(content(sitemap_resp, as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8"))
    23→    all_urls <- xml_find_all(sitemap_xml, ".//loc") %>% xml_text()
    24→
    25→    # Filter for URLs that contain /learning_center/racquet_reviews/
    26→    sitemap_links <- all_urls[str_detect(all_urls, "/learning_center/racquet_reviews/")]
    27→  } else {
    28→    message("Sitemap not found or inaccessible.")
    29→  }
    30→
    31→  # We'll create a tibble for these. We don't have brand/model from the sitemap,
    32→  # so set them to NA. We can parse the 'code' from the URL, but keep in mind
    33→  # the substring might not match the gtm_impression_code from the main page exactly.
    34→  df_sitemap <- tibble(
    35→    model = NA_character_,
    36→    brand = NA_character_,
    37→    # Example code extraction (if you want a code column):
    38→    code  = str_extract(sitemap_links, "(?<=racquet_reviews/)[^/]+(?=.html)"),
    39→    link  = sitemap_links
    40→  )
    41→
    42→  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    43→  # 2) Scrape the main "reviewedracquets.html" page for brand, model, code
    44→  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    45→  main_page_url <- "https://www.tennis-warehouse.com/reviewedracquets.html"
    46→  main_page <- tryCatch(read_html(main_page_url), error = function(e) NULL)
    47→
    48→  df_main <- tibble(model = character(0), brand = character(0), code = character(0), link = character(0))
    49→
    50→  if (!is.null(main_page)) {
    51→    # Extract brand, model, code from data-gtm_impression attributes
    52→    impression_nodes <- main_page %>% html_nodes("div.cattable-wrap-cell.gtm_impression")
    53→
    54→    brands <- impression_nodes %>% html_attr("data-gtm_impression_brand")
    55→    models <- impression_nodes %>% html_attr("data-gtm_impression_name")
    56→    codes  <- impression_nodes %>% html_attr("data-gtm_impression_code")
    57→
    58→    # Extract actual links from the relevant container
    59→    link_nodes <- main_page %>%
    60→      html_nodes("div.cattable-wrap-cell-imgwrap.is-racquet") %>%
    61→      html_nodes("a") %>%
    62→      html_attr("href")
    63→
    64→    links <- paste0("https://www.tennis-warehouse.com", link_nodes)
    65→
    66→    # Combine into a tibble
    67→    df_main <- tibble(
    68→      model = models,
    69→      brand = brands,
    70→      code  = codes,
    71→      link  = links
    72→    )
    73→  } else {
    74→    message("Failed to scrape main reviews page.")
    75→  }
    76→
    77→  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    78→  # 3) Combine both sets of results, remove duplicates, clean the 'link' column
    79→  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    80→  df_out <- bind_rows(df_sitemap, df_main) %>%
    81→    mutate(
    82→      # Remove any carriage returns/newlines from link,
    83→      # then trim any leading/trailing whitespace.
    84→      link = link %>%
    85→        str_replace_all("[\r\n]", "") %>%
    86→        str_trim()
    87→    ) %>%
    88→    distinct(link, .keep_all = TRUE)
    89→
    90→  return(df_out)
    91→}
    92→

    94→get_clean_html <- function(url) {
    95→  # Minimal placeholder for your existing logic...
    96→  tryCatch(
    97→    xml2::read_html(url),
    98→    error = function(e) {
    99→      message("Error reading URL: ", url, " => ", e$message)
   100→      return(NULL)
   101→    }
   102→  )
   103→}
   104→
   105→
   106→get_clean_html <- function(url) {
   107→  # Minimal placeholder logic. Could handle user agents, retries, etc.
   108→  tryCatch(
   109→    { xml2::read_html(url) },
   110→    error = function(e) {
   111→      message("Error reading URL: ", url)
   112→      return(NULL)
   113→    }
   114→  )
   115→}
   116→
   117→gather_intel_on_review_page <- function(url) {
   118→  doc <- get_clean_html(url)
   119→  if (is.null(doc)) {
   120→    return(tibble(
   121→      link = url,
   122→      ok   = FALSE,
   123→      # You can track as many features as you want, here is just an example set:
   124→      has_div_scores           = NA_integer_,
   125→      has_div_review_scores    = NA_integer_,
   126→      has_div_scores_table     = NA_integer_,
   127→      has_div_score_box        = NA_integer_,
   128→      has_old_specs_table      = NA_integer_,
   129→      has_racquet_rate_table   = NA_integer_,
   130→      has_breakdown_summary_id = NA_integer_,
   131→      has_playtester_sections  = NA_integer_,
   132→      has_new_breakdown_table  = NA_integer_,
   133→      has_new_twu_labdata      = NA_integer_,
   134→      has_new_tech_specs       = NA_integer_
   135→    ))
   136→  }
   137→
   138→  # ============ OLD-STYLE checks ============
   139→  ds        <- doc %>% html_nodes("div.scores")
   140→  ds2       <- doc %>% html_nodes("div.review_scores")
   141→  ds_tab    <- doc %>% html_nodes("div.scores table")
   142→  ds_sbox   <- doc %>% html_nodes("div.score_box")
   143→  old_specs <- doc %>% html_nodes("table.racquet_specs_table")
   144→  rrate     <- doc %>% html_nodes("table.racquet_rate_table")
   145→  breakdown <- doc %>% html_nodes("#breakdown_summary")
   146→  # or doc %>% html_nodes("#breakdown_summary table")
   147→
   148→  # ============ NEW-STYLE checks ============
   149→  # E.g. "div#table.table-responsive.review-table table" might be the new breakdown
   150→  new_breakdown <- doc %>% html_nodes("div#table.table-responsive.review-table table")
   151→  # TWU lab data in #table_data
   152→  new_lab_data  <- doc %>% html_nodes("#table_data table")
   153→  # Tech specs in #tech_specs or #US / #EU
   154→  new_techspecs <- doc %>% html_nodes("#tech_specs table")
   155→
   156→  # The .review-playtesters_section blocks for the new style:
   157→  ptesters <- doc %>% html_nodes(".review-playtesters_section")
   158→
   159→  tibble(
   160→    link       = url,
   161→    ok         = TRUE,
   162→    # OLD
   163→    has_div_scores        = length(ds),
   164→    has_div_review_scores = length(ds2),
   165→    has_div_scores_table  = length(ds_tab),
   166→    has_div_score_box     = length(ds_sbox),
   167→    has_old_specs_table   = length(old_specs),
   168→    has_racquet_rate_table= length(rrate),
   169→    has_breakdown_summary_id = length(breakdown),
   170→
   171→    # NEW
   172→    has_playtester_sections = length(ptesters),
   173→    has_new_breakdown_table = length(new_breakdown),
   174→    has_new_twu_labdata     = length(new_lab_data),
   175→    has_new_tech_specs      = length(new_techspecs)
   176→  )
   177→}
   178→
   179→
   180→# Optional wrapper if you want to do many links at once:
   181→gather_intel_on_many_pages <- function(urls) {
   182→  # lapply or purrr::map over the set of links
   183→  result_list <- lapply(urls, gather_intel_on_review_page)
   184→  bind_rows(result_list)
   185→}

   191→library(purrr)
   192→
   193→library(rvest)
   194→library(dplyr)
   195→library(stringr)
   196→library(purrr)
   197→
   198→parse_new_style <- function(doc) {
   199→  # We'll store both the staff "breakdown summary" plus each individual's breakdown
   200→  out_list <- list()
   201→
   202→  # STEP 1: Gather all "table.table-borderless" nodes from the main review container.
   203→  #         We do want to skip "Tech Specs" tables and "Lab Data" tables.
   204→  candidate_tables <- doc %>%
   205→    html_node("div.review-body_container") %>% # or "div.review-container"
   206→    html_nodes("table.table.table-borderless")
   207→
   208→  if (length(candidate_tables) == 0) {
   209→    return(tibble())
   210→  }
   211→
   212→  # We'll parse each table and see if it looks like a "score table"
   213→  # vs. a "tech specs" vs. a "lab data" or "playtester" table.
   214→  out_all <- map_dfr(candidate_tables, function(tbl) {
   215→
   216→    # Quick text check to see if this table is obviously Tech Specs or Lab Data
   217→    # If it has "Length" / "Head Size" / "Flex Rating" in the first row, skip.
   218→    all_text <- tbl %>% html_text()
   219→    if (grepl("(Length|Flex Rating|Swing Weight|Head Size)", all_text, ignore.case=TRUE)) {
   220→      # we skip "tech specs" or "lab data" tables
   221→      return(tibble())
   222→    }
   223→
   224→    # If not a Tech Specs table, we proceed to see if it's the staff or a player table.
   225→    rows <- tbl %>% html_nodes("tr")
   226→    if (length(rows) == 0) return(tibble())
   227→
   228→    # The first row might say "Groundstrokes" or "Michelle's Breakdown" or "Chris' Breakdown"...
   229→    # or it might have category/score pairs directly.
   230→    # We'll see if the FIRST row has a single <td colspan="2"> with text like "Chris's Breakdown".
   231→    # If so, we parse from row 2 onward for the category/score pairs.
   232→
   233→    # We'll read the entire text of the first <tr>:
   234→    first_tr_text <- rows[[1]] %>% html_text(trim=TRUE)
   235→
   236→    # If that first line says something like "Michelle's Breakdown" or "Troy's Breakdown", etc.
   237→    # we can parse out the name.
   238→    # We'll do a simple pattern:
   239→    # e.g. "Michelle's Breakdown" => "Michelle"
   240→    # If it does NOT match that pattern, maybe it's the "Breakdown Summary" (the staff row).
   241→    # We'll guess "TW Staff" for that scenario.
   242→
   243→    # We also might see "Groundstrokes  8.3" right away if the first row is already a category row.
   244→    # So let’s see if the first row is a single cell with "someone's breakdown" or if it’s 2 cells.
   245→
   246→    tds_first_tr <- rows[[1]] %>% html_nodes("td")
   247→    if (length(tds_first_tr) == 1) {
   248→      # Probably something like "Michelle's Breakdown" in one big colspan.
   249→      # We'll parse out the name:
   250→      # e.g. "Michelle's Breakdown"
   251→      # We'll strip off "'s Breakdown"
   252→      possible_name <- first_tr_text
   253→      # remove trailing "’s Breakdown" or "’ Scores"
   254→      possible_name <- str_remove(possible_name, "'?s Breakdown")
   255→      possible_name <- str_remove(possible_name, "'?s Scores")
   256→      # handle any leftover
   257→      possible_name <- str_squish(possible_name)
   258→
   259→      # Now parse subsequent rows as category/score pairs
   260→      if (length(rows) > 1) {
   261→        # the real data starts at row 2
   262→        row_data <- rows[-1]
   263→      } else {
   264→        return(tibble())
   265→      }
   266→    } else {
   267→      # This might be the staff table, or it might simply start with a category row
   268→      # We'll call the player "TW Staff" for that scenario
   269→      # (or we can set "unknown" until we see if there's a "Final Verdict" row)
   270→      possible_name <- "TW Staff"
   271→      row_data <- rows
   272→    }
   273→
   274→    # Now parse each row in row_data
   275→    # Usually each row has 2 columns: category, score
   276→    # final verdict row might say "Final Verdict" and "8.3"
   277→    data_rows <- map_dfr(row_data, function(rr) {
   278→      tds <- rr %>% html_nodes("td")
   279→      if (length(tds) == 2) {
   280→        cat_text   <- html_text(tds[[1]], trim=TRUE)
   281→        score_text <- html_text(tds[[2]], trim=TRUE)
   282→        tibble(
   283→          player   = possible_name,
   284→          category = cat_text,
   285→          score    = score_text
   286→        )
   287→      } else {
   288→        tibble()
   289→      }
   290→    })
   291→
   292→    data_rows
   293→  })
   294→
   295→  # That should combine staff + each individual's table (unless we found "tech specs" or "lab data").
   296→  out_all
   297→}
   298→
   299→
   300→
   301→# parse main "TW Staff" summary table from old style (div.scores)
   302→parse_main_scores_oldstyle <- function(doc) {
   303→  out <- tibble()
   304→  main_tbl <- doc %>% html_node("div.scores table")
   305→  if (!is.null(main_tbl)) {
   306→    rows <- main_tbl %>% html_nodes("tr")
   307→    out_list <- lapply(rows, function(rr) {
   308→      tds <- rr %>% html_nodes("th, td") %>% html_text(trim=TRUE)
   309→      if (length(tds)==2) {
   310→        tibble(player="TW Staff", category=tds[1], score=tds[2])
   311→      } else {
   312→        NULL
   313→      }
   314→    })
   315→    out <- bind_rows(out_list)
   316→  }
   317→  out
   318→}
   319→
   320→# parse old style "table.playscore" for individual testers
   321→parse_individual_scores_oldstyle <- function(doc) {
   322→  out <- tibble()
   323→  playscore_tables <- doc %>% html_nodes("table.playscore")
   324→  if (length(playscore_tables) == 0) return(out)
   325→
   326→  all_data <- lapply(playscore_tables, function(tbl) {
   327→    # get player name from top row
   328→    first_th <- tbl %>% html_node("tr th")
   329→    if (is.null(first_th)) return(NULL)
   330→    heading_text <- html_text(first_th, trim=TRUE)
   331→    # e.g., "Chris' Scores" -> "Chris"
   332→    # you can just store the raw heading_text, or strip out " Scores"
   333→    heading_text <- sub(" Scores$", "", heading_text)
   334→
   335→    # parse subsequent rows
   336→    rows_ <- tbl %>% html_nodes("tr")
   337→    # typically first row is the heading row -> skip
   338→    if (length(rows_) > 1) rows_ <- rows_[-1]
   339→    out_each <- lapply(rows_, function(rr) {
   340→      tds <- rr %>% html_nodes("td") %>% html_text(trim=TRUE)
   341→      # tds might be c("Power","8.4","Serves","8.7")
   342→      # parse them in pairs
   343→      small_list <- list()
   344→      if (length(tds) >= 2) {
   345→        small_list[[1]] <- tibble(
   346→          player=heading_text,
   347→          category=tds[1],
   348→          score=tds[2]
   349→        )
   350→      }
   351→      if (length(tds) >= 4) {
   352→        small_list[[2]] <- tibble(
   353→          player=heading_text,
   354→          category=tds[3],
   355→          score=tds[4]
   356→        )
   357→      }
   358→      bind_rows(small_list)
   359→    })
   360→    bind_rows(out_each)
   361→  })
   362→
   363→  out <- bind_rows(all_data)
   364→  out
   365→}
   366→
   367→parse_racquet_specs <- function(doc) {
   368→  length_        <- NA_character_
   369→  head_size_     <- NA_character_
   370→  weight_        <- NA_character_
   371→  balance_       <- NA_character_
   372→  construction_  <- NA_character_
   373→  material_      <- NA_character_
   374→  string_pattern <- NA_character_
   375→
   376→  # ---------------------------
   377→  # 1) Attempt OLD-STYLE table
   378→  # ---------------------------
   379→  specs_tbl <- doc %>% rvest::html_node("table.racquet_specs_table")
   380→  if (!is.null(specs_tbl)) {
   381→    rows <- specs_tbl %>% rvest::html_nodes("tr")
   382→    for (rr in rows) {
   383→      cols <- rr %>% rvest::html_nodes("th, td") %>% rvest::html_text(trim=TRUE)
   384→      if (length(cols) >= 2) {
   385→        label <- cols[1]
   386→        val   <- cols[2]
   387→        if (grepl("^Length", label, ignore.case=TRUE))            length_        <- val
   388→        if (grepl("^Head Size", label, ignore.case=TRUE))         head_size_     <- val
   389→        if (grepl("^Weight", label, ignore.case=TRUE))            weight_        <- val
   390→        if (grepl("^Balance", label, ignore.case=TRUE))           balance_       <- val
   391→        if (grepl("^Construction", label, ignore.case=TRUE))      construction_  <- val
   392→        if (grepl("String Pattern", label, ignore.case=TRUE))     string_pattern <- val
   393→        if (grepl("Composition|Material", label, ignore.case=TRUE)) material_     <- val
   394→      }
   395→    }
   396→  }
   397→
   398→  # -----------------------------
   399→  # 2) Attempt “New Style” specs
   400→  #    if we still have all NA
   401→  # -----------------------------
   402→  # Some pages have class="review-techspecs",
   403→  # others have class="review-techspec" (singular).
   404→  # Also note #US is often in a sibling or child node.
   405→  if (all(is.na(c(length_, head_size_, weight_, balance_, construction_, material_, string_pattern)))) {
   406→    new_style_tbl <- doc %>%
   407→      # This selector says: find EITHER div.review-techspec OR div.review-techspecs
   408→      html_node("div.review-techspec, div.review-techspecs") %>%
   409→      # Then within that, look for #US table.table-borderless
   410→      html_node("#US table.table-borderless")
   411→
   412→    if (!is.null(new_style_tbl)) {
   413→      rows <- new_style_tbl %>% rvest::html_nodes("tr")
   414→      for (rr in rows) {
   415→        cols <- rr %>% rvest::html_nodes("td") %>% rvest::html_text(trim=TRUE)
   416→        if (length(cols) >= 2) {
   417→          label <- cols[1]
   418→          val   <- cols[2]
   419→          if (grepl("^Length", label, ignore.case=TRUE))            length_        <- val
   420→          if (grepl("^Head Size", label, ignore.case=TRUE))         head_size_     <- val
   421→          if (grepl("^Weight", label, ignore.case=TRUE))            weight_        <- val
   422→          if (grepl("^Balance", label, ignore.case=TRUE))           balance_       <- val
   423→          if (grepl("^Construction", label, ignore.case=TRUE))      construction_  <- val
   424→          if (grepl("String Pattern", label, ignore.case=TRUE))     string_pattern <- val
   425→          if (grepl("Composition|Material", label, ignore.case=TRUE)) material_     <- val
   426→        }
   427→      }
   428→    }
   429→  }
   430→
   431→  list(
   432→    length        = length_,
   433→    head_size     = head_size_,
   434→    weight        = weight_,
   435→    balance       = balance_,
   436→    construction  = construction_,
   437→    material      = material_,
   438→    string_pattern= string_pattern
   439→  )
   440→}
   441→
   442→parse_racquet_rdc <- function(doc) {
   443→  flex <- NA_real_
   444→  swingweight <- NA_real_
   445→
   446→  # ---------------------------
   447→  # 1) Attempt OLD-STYLE <table.racquet_rate_table>
   448→  # ---------------------------
   449→  rdc_tbl <- doc %>% rvest::html_node("table.racquet_rate_table")
   450→  if (!is.null(rdc_tbl)) {
   451→    rows <- rdc_tbl %>% rvest::html_nodes("tr")
   452→    for (rr in rows) {
   453→      tds <- rr %>% rvest::html_nodes("th, td") %>% rvest::html_text(trim=TRUE)
   454→      # e.g.: c("Flex Rating","65","Range: 0-100")
   455→      # or   c("Swing Weight","322","Range: 200-400")
   456→      if (length(tds) >= 2) {
   457→        if (grepl("Flex Rating", tds[1], ignore.case=TRUE)) {
   458→          flex <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(tds[2]))
   459→        }
   460→        if (grepl("Swing Weight", tds[1], ignore.case=TRUE)) {
   461→          swingweight <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(tds[2]))
   462→        }
   463→      }
   464→    }
   465→  }
   466→
   467→  # ---------------------------
   468→  # 2) NEW-STYLE Variations (Partial Overwrite)
   469→  # ---------------------------
   470→  # If either flex or swingweight is still NA, check new layouts
   471→  if (is.na(flex) || is.na(swingweight)) {
   472→    # Try multiple selectors for new-style tables
   473→    selectors <- c("table#table_data", ".review-table_data table", ".table-responsive.review-table table")
   474→    new_lab_tbl <- NULL
   475→    for (sel in selectors) {
   476→      new_lab_tbl <- doc %>% rvest::html_node(sel)
   477→      if (!is.na(new_lab_tbl)) break
   478→    }
   479→
   480→    if (!is.na(new_lab_tbl)) {
   481→      rows <- new_lab_tbl %>% rvest::html_nodes("tr")
   482→      for (rr in rows) {
   483→        tds <- rr %>% rvest::html_nodes("td") %>% rvest::html_text(trim=TRUE)
   484→        # e.g. c("Flex Rating", "66", "MEDIUM")
   485→        #      c("Swing Weight", "314", "LOW")
   486→        if (length(tds) >= 2) {
   487→          if (grepl("Flex Rating", tds[1], ignore.case=TRUE)) {
   488→            # only overwrite if flex is NA
   489→            if (is.na(flex)) {
   490→              flex <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(tds[2]))
   491→            }
   492→          }
   493→          if (grepl("Swing Weight", tds[1], ignore.case=TRUE)) {
   494→            # only overwrite if swingweight is NA
   495→            if (is.na(swingweight)) {
   496→              swingweight <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(tds[2]))
   497→            }
   498→          }
   499→        }
   500→      }
   501→    }
   502→  }
   503→
   504→  if (is.na(flex)) {
   505→    browser()
   506→  }
   507→
   508→  list(flex = flex, swingweight = swingweight)
   509→}
   510→
   511→parse_racquet_review <- function(intelligence_row, model, brand, code) {
   512→  link <- intelligence_row[["link"]]
   513→  message("===== Parsing racquet review for link: ", link)
   514→
   515→  doc <- get_clean_html(link)
   516→  if (is.null(doc)) {
   517→    warning("Failed to retrieve HTML for link: ", link)
   518→    return(tibble())
   519→  }
   520→
   521→  # 1) Try old-style parse for scores
   522→  oldstyle_main <- parse_main_scores_oldstyle(doc)
   523→  oldstyle_individual <- parse_individual_scores_oldstyle(doc)
   524→
   525→  # 2) If both old style calls returned empty, check new style
   526→  all_scores <- bind_rows(oldstyle_main, oldstyle_individual)
   527→  if (nrow(all_scores) == 0) {
   528→    newstyle_scores <- parse_new_style(doc)
   529→    all_scores <- newstyle_scores
   530→  }
   531→
   532→  if (nrow(all_scores) == 0) {
   533→    message("No scores found. Returning empty tibble.")
   534→    return(tibble())
   535→  }
   536→
   537→  # 3) Parse specs & RDC
   538→  specs_list <- parse_racquet_specs(doc)
   539→  rdc_list   <- parse_racquet_rdc(doc)
   540→
   541→  # 4) Return combined
   542→  out <- all_scores %>%
   543→    mutate(
   544→      model  = model,
   545→      code   = code,
   546→      brand  = brand,
   547→      flex         = rdc_list$flex,
   548→      swingweight  = rdc_list$swingweight,
   549→      length       = specs_list$length,
   550→      head_size    = specs_list$head_size,
   551→      weight       = specs_list$weight,
   552→      balance      = specs_list$balance,
   553→      construction = specs_list$construction,
   554→      material     = specs_list$material,
   555→      string_pattern = specs_list$string_pattern
   556→    ) %>%
   557→    select(player, category, score,
   558→           model, code, brand,
   559→           flex, swingweight, length, head_size, weight,
   560→           balance, construction, material, string_pattern)
   561→
   562→  message("===== Done parsing link: ", link,
   563→          " --> found ", nrow(out), " total scores.")
   564→  return(out)
   565→}
   566→
   567→post_process_review <- function(df) {
   568→  library(dplyr)
   569→  library(stringr)
   570→
   571→  df <- df %>%
   572→    mutate(
   573→      #################################################################
   574→      ## 1) Convert score to numeric on a 0–10 scale
   575→      #################################################################
   576→      # - Convert to numeric if possible
   577→      # - If score > 10, assume it was out of 100 and divide by 10
   578→      # - Otherwise leave it as-is
   579→      score = as.numeric(score),
   580→      score = case_when(
   581→        is.na(score) ~ NA_real_,
   582→        score > 10   ~ score / 10,
   583→        TRUE         ~ score
   584→      ),
   585→
   586→      #################################################################
   587→      ## 2) Parse numeric columns from strings
   588→      #################################################################
   589→
   590→      # ----- Weight (e.g. "11.2 oz", "10.7 oz") -----
   591→      # We'll extract up to the first numeric part, ignoring "oz".
   592→      # If unparseable, we get NA.
   593→      weight_numeric = as.numeric(str_extract(weight, "[0-9\\.]+")),
   594→
   595→      # ----- Length (e.g. "27.5 in", "27.5 inch") -----
   596→      # We'll likewise extract the first numeric portion.
   597→      length_numeric = as.numeric(str_extract(length, "[0-9\\.]+")),
   598→
   599→      # ----- Head size (e.g. "100 sq in", "98", "95 in", etc.) -----
   600→      # We look for numeric portion. If unparseable => NA.
   601→      head_size_numeric = as.numeric(str_extract(head_size, "[0-9\\.]+")),
   602→
   603→      # ----- Balance (inches from butt)
   604→      # We'll parse the first floating number. If none => NA.
   605→      balance_in = as.numeric(str_extract(balance, "[0-9\\.]+")),
   606→
   607→      #################################################################
   608→      ## 3) Clean up / unify string_pattern to "16x19", "16x20", etc.
   609→      #################################################################
   610→      # We'll do a series of replacements to unify forms like:
   611→      #   "16 / 19", "16x19", "16 x 19", "16 Mains / 19 Crosses", etc.
   612→      #
   613→      # Then we keep only digits + 'x' in the final pattern. E.g. "16x19".
   614→      # If you have patterns like "19x19" or "14x18", you can keep them,
   615→      # or define them in the replacements below.
   616→      #
   617→      # We do everything in one go using "case_when" or stepwise gsub.
   618→      # Because there are multiple forms, a small chain of gsub often is easiest.
   619→      #################################################################
   620→      pattern_cleaned = string_pattern %>%
   621→        # Convert "mains" or "Mains" or "crosses" or "Crosses" => remove them
   622→        str_replace_all("(?i)mains|crosses", "") %>%
   623→        # Convert "x", " X ", " / ", etc. to a single "x"
   624→        str_replace_all("(?i)[^0-9]+", "x") %>%    # anything not a digit => "x"
   625→        # This might result in patterns like "16x19x" if there is a trailing character.
   626→        # We'll trim trailing x if present
   627→        str_remove_all("x$") %>%
   628→        # If we had "16x18x19" that might be a problem, so keep only the first two groups of digits:
   629→        # We'll do a quick fix: keep only the first 2 groups of digits separated by 'x'
   630→        # e.g. "16x19x" => "16x19"
   631→        # For safety, let's do a more explicit approach:
   632→        str_extract("^\\d{1,2}x\\d{1,2}")  # e.g. "16x19" or "18x20" or "16x18"
   633→
   634→      # You could do more advanced logic if your data has weird outliers.
   635→      # Or you can coalesce to NA if unmatched.
   636→    ) %>%
   637→    # Because we may want a single combined "string density" that merges head_size and pattern
   638→    # we can create it after the above mutate. E.g. "100in_16x19"
   639→    mutate(
   640→      string_density = if_else(
   641→        !is.na(head_size_numeric) & !is.na(pattern_cleaned),
   642→        paste0(head_size_numeric, "in_", pattern_cleaned),
   643→        NA_character_
   644→      )
   645→    )
   646→
   647→  df %>%
   648→    mutate(
   649→      # First parse numeric from 'balance' and store in inches
   650→      balance_in = as.numeric(str_extract(balance, "[0-9\\.]+")),
   651→      # Also parse numeric from 'length'. If missing, fallback to 27 inches:
   652→      length_in  = as.numeric(str_extract(length, "[0-9\\.]+")),
   653→      length_in  = if_else(is.na(length_in), 27, length_in),
   654→
   655→      # Compute midpoint in inches
   656→      midpoint_in = length_in / 2,
   657→
   658→      # Distance from midpoint (if positive => racquet is head-light)
   659→      balance_diff_in = midpoint_in - balance_in,
   660→
   661→      # Convert that difference to "points" (1 point = 1/8 inch)
   662→      # Round to nearest integer for typical labeling
   663→      balance_pts_num = round(8 * abs(balance_diff_in)),
   664→
   665→      # Finally, create a text label such as "4 pts HL" or "2 pts HH"
   666→      # or "EB" if difference is near zero
   667→      balance_pts = case_when(
   668→        is.na(balance_in) ~ NA_character_,
   669→        abs(balance_diff_in) < 0.05 ~ "EB",  # small threshold for even
   670→        balance_diff_in > 0        ~ paste0(balance_pts_num, " pts HL"),
   671→        TRUE                       ~ paste0(balance_pts_num, " pts HH")
   672→      )
   673→    )
   674→}
   675→
   676→
   677→intel = gather_review_pages_intel(discover_racquet_reviews()$link)
   678→revs <- discover_racquet_reviews()
   679→
   680→
   681→# 1) Combine the intel info with the discovered reviews info
   682→df_combined <- left_join(intel, revs, by = "link")
   683→
   684→# 2) Iterate (map) over each row, calling parse_racquet_review()
   685→#    The function returns a tibble, so we use map_dfr() to row-bind them all.
   686→all_review_data <- map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(df_combined)), function(i) {
   687→  print(i)
   688→  row_i <- df_combined[i, ]
   689→  ret <- parse_racquet_review(
   690→    intelligence_row = row_i,
   691→    model = row_i$model,
   692→    brand = row_i$brand,
   693→    code  = row_i$code
   694→  )
   695→  print(ret$weight[1])
   696→  print(ret$flex[1])
   697→
   698→
   699→  ret
   700→})
   701→
   702→# all_review_data is your final data frame of racquet review info
   703→processed_data <- post_process_review(all_review_data)
   704→
   705→
   706→parse_review_text <- function(link) {
   707→
   708→  doc <- tryCatch({
   709→    read_html(link)
   710→  }, error = function(e) {
   711→    warning("Could not read HTML: ", e$message)
   712→    return(NULL)
   713→  })
   714→
   715→  if (is.null(doc)) {
   716→    return(list(
   717→      summary_text = NA_character_,
   718→      body_text = NA_character_,
   719→      likes_text = NA_character_,
   720→      dislikes_text = NA_character_,
   721→      comparing_text = NA_character_,
   722→      playtester_profiles = NA_character_
   723→    ))
   724→  }
   725→
   726→  # Helper function: gather text from a set of nodes
   727→  # and return as one text block
   728→  node_text_block <- function(html_nodeset) {
   729→    text_vec <- html_nodeset %>%
   730→      html_text2() %>%            # get text (html_text2 has no trim arg)
   731→      str_trim(side = "both")     # strip leading/trailing whitespace
   732→    paste(text_vec, collapse = "\n\n")
   733→  }
   734→
   735→  # Extract summary from 'div.review_breakdown'
   736→  summary_node <- doc %>% html_node("div.review_breakdown")
   737→  summary_text <- if (!is.null(summary_node)) {
   738→    # get paragraphs + list items
   739→    node_text_block(summary_node %>% html_nodes("p, li"))
   740→  } else NA_character_
   741→
   742→  # Extract main body from 'div.review_body'
   743→  body_node <- doc %>% html_node("div.review_body")
   744→  body_text <- if (!is.null(body_node)) {
   745→    node_text_block(body_node %>% html_nodes("p"))
   746→  } else NA_character_
   747→
   748→  # Extract Likes / Dislikes / Comparing from 'div.review_comments'
   749→  likes_text     <- NA_character_
   750→  dislikes_text  <- NA_character_
   751→  comparing_text <- NA_character_
   752→
   753→  comments_node <- doc %>% html_node("div.review_comments")
   754→  if (!is.null(comments_node)) {
   755→    comment_boxes <- comments_node %>% html_nodes("div.review_comment_box")
   756→    for (cb in comment_boxes) {
   757→      heading <- cb %>% html_node("h2") %>% html_text2() %>% str_trim()
   758→      cblock_txt <- node_text_block(cb %>% html_nodes("p"))
   759→
   760→      if (grepl("likes", heading, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
   761→        likes_text <- cblock_txt
   762→      } else if (grepl("dislike", heading, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
   763→        dislikes_text <- cblock_txt
   764→      } else if (grepl("Comparing the racquet", heading, ignore.case=TRUE)) {
   765→        comparing_text <- cblock_txt
   766→      }
   767→    }
   768→  }
   769→
   770→  # Extract playtester profiles from 'div.playtest_profiles'
   771→  playtest_profiles_node <- doc %>% html_node("div.playtest_profiles")
   772→  playtester_profiles <- if (!is.null(playtest_profiles_node)) {
   773→    node_text_block(playtest_profiles_node %>% html_nodes("p"))
   774→  } else NA_character_
   775→
   776→  # Return a list with all captured text
   777→  list(
   778→    summary_text         = summary_text,
   779→    body_text            = body_text,
   780→    likes_text           = likes_text,
   781→    dislikes_text        = dislikes_text,
   782→    comparing_text       = comparing_text,
   783→    playtester_profiles  = playtester_profiles
   784→  )
   785→}

    31→  svals <- read_html(link) %>% html_nodes("div.scores tr td") %>% html_text()
    32→  if (length(snames) == 0) {
    33→    sall <- read_html(link) %>% html_nodes("div.review_scores tr td") %>% html_text()
    34→
    35→    if (length(sall) != 0) {
    36→      snames <- sall[seq(1,length(sall), by=2)]
    37→      svals <- sall[seq(2,length(sall), by=2)]
    38→    }
    39→  }
    40→
    41→  if (length(snames) == 0) {
    42→    sall <- read_html(link) %>% html_nodes("div.scores table tr td") %>% html_text()
    43→
    44→    if (length(sall) != 0) {
    45→      snames <- sall[seq(1,length(sall), by=2)]
    46→      svals <- sall[seq(2,length(sall), by=2)]

    48→
    49→    if (length(sall) == 0) {

    51→
    52→      if (length(sall) != 0) {

    55→      }
    56→    }

    58→
    59→
    60→  rdc_ratings <- read_html(link) %>% html_nodes("table.racquet_rate_table") %>% html_nodes("td") %>% html_text2(preserve_nbsp = TRUE)
    61→  if (length(rdc_ratings) == 0) {
    62→    rdc_ratings <- read_html(link) %>% html_nodes("table td table") %>% html_nodes("font") %>% html_text2(preserve_nbsp = TRUE)
    63→    ind1 <- grep("Flex Rating", rdc_ratings)
    64→    ind2 <- grep("Swing Weight", rdc_ratings)
    65→    rdc_ratings <- rdc_ratings[c(ind1+1, ind2+1)]
    66→  } else {
    67→    tval <- as.numeric(rdc_ratings[2])
    68→    if (!is.na(tval) && tval < 80 & tval > 40) {

    70→    } else {
    71→      ind1 <- grep("Flex Rating", rdc_ratings)
    72→      ind2 <- grep("Swing Weight", rdc_ratings)

    74→    }
    75→  }

    77→  print(rdc_ratings)

    79→  specs <- read_html(link) %>% html_nodes("table.racquet_specs_table") %>% html_nodes("tr td") %>% html_text()
    80→  if (length(specs) == 0) {
    81→     specs <- read_html(link) %>% html_nodes("#main_content > center > table > tr > td:nth-child(1)") %>% html_text2(preserve_nbsp = TRUE)
    82→     specs<- try(stringr::str_split(specs, "[\t\n]")[[1]])
    83→
    84→     if (inherits(specs, "try-error")) {
    85→       specs <- read_html(link) %>% html_nodes("center > table > tr > td:nth-child(1)") %>% html_text2(preserve_nbsp = TRUE)
    86→       specs<- try(stringr::str_split(specs, "[\t\n]")[[1]])
    87→      }
    88→  }
    89→
    90→  indiv <- read_html(link) %>% html_nodes("div.individ_scores") %>% html_nodes("div.tablewrap") %>% html_nodes("table")
    91→  if (length(indiv) == 0) {
    92→    indiv <- read_html(link) %>% html_nodes(".tablewrap")  %>% html_nodes("table")
    93→  }

    95→  indiv <- lapply(indiv, function(x) {
    96→    sname <- x %>% html_nodes("tr th") %>% html_text()
    97→    sname <- iconv(sname, from = 'UTF-8', to = 'ASCII//TRANSLIT')
    98→    sname <- gsub("Chris' Scores", "Chris", sname)
    99→    sname <- gsub("Tiffani' Scores", "Tiffani", sname)
   100→    sname <- gsub("James' Scores", "James", sname)
   101→    sname <- gsub("Z'sofia", "Zsofi", sname)
   102→    sname <- gsub("'$", "", sname)

   104→    sval <- x %>% html_nodes("tr td") %>% html_text()
   105→
   106→    category <- c("Power", "Serves", "Control", "Groundstrokes", "Maneuverability", "Returns",
   107→               "Stability", "Slice", "Comfort", "Topspin", "Touch/Feel", "Volleys")
   108→    scores <- sapply(category, function(cat) {
